title: Preparation of RNAse Free 4% Paraformaldehyde in 0.1M PB
author: Monique Copeland

steps.title: Add 400 mL of Nuclease-free water...
steps.instruction: Add 400 mL of Nuclease-free water to a 1000 mL, beaker

steps.title: Add stir bar in beaker and...
steps.instruction: Add stir bar in beaker and set on hot plate at 50 °C

steps.title: Add 5 drops (500ul) of 10N...
steps.instruction: Add 5 drops (500ul) of 10N NaOH (from 60ml dropper) Sodium Hydroxide, 10.0N Thomas Scientific Catalog #C871W32 (this makes the solution basic to help dissolve the paraformaldehyde)

steps.title: Weigh 20 g of Paraformaldehyde and add to MilliQ water
steps.instruction: Weigh 20 g of Paraformaldehyde and add to MilliQ water. (Stir about 01:00:00 or until all parafromaldehyde is dissolved and solution is clear) Paraformaldehyde Contributed by users Catalog #19200

steps.title: Make 1 Molarity (M) Phosphate buffer at PH 7
steps.instruction: Make 1 Molarity (M) Phosphate buffer at PH 7.4

steps.title: Top off to 500ml with MilliQ water
steps.instruction: Top off to 500ml with MilliQ water.

steps.title: Filter solution through
steps.instruction: Filter solution through .2um CA member filter. Corning™ Disposable Vacuum Filter/Storage Systems Fisher Scientific Catalog #09-761-5

steps.title: Label container with 'RNAse free 4% paraformaldehyed in
steps.instruction: Label container with 'RNAse free 4% paraformaldehyed in .1M PB' and date. Store at 4 °C, for up to 1 week. (Can also be aliquoted into Falcon Tube (50 mL) Fischer Scientific and stored at -20 °C for 3 months.)

steps.title: To make 100 mL, 1M PB at PH 7
steps.instruction: To make 100 mL, 1M PB at PH 7.4 add 19 mL, 1M Sodium phosphate Monobasic plus 81 mL, 1M Sodium Dibasic dihydrate (store as stock at Room temperature ) Sodium Phosphate Dibasic Dihydrate (USP/FCC/EP/BP), Fisher Chemical Fisher Scientific Catalog #S472-500 Sodium Phosphate Dibasic Dihydrate (USP/FCC/EP/BP), Fisher Chemical Fisher Scientific Catalog #S472-500

steps.title: Add 50 mL, 1M PB PH 7
steps.instruction: Add 50 mL, 1M PB PH 7.4 to paraformaldehyde solution.

steps.title: Test PH and adjust solution to final desired 7
steps.instruction: Test PH and adjust solution to final desired 7.4pH using HCl or NaOH.
